steelman_no_trade.draft: A rational Senior could pass because the base case already assumes healthy durable revenue growth and margin discipline, leaving limited room for error if device demand or services growth disappoints.
steelman_no_trade.excerpt_or_summary: The filed scenario set and valuation range already embed optimistic operating assumptions.

counterparty.draft: The plausible counterparty is a benchmark-constrained large-cap holder reducing exposure because AAPL's scale makes incremental growth harder to underwrite versus faster index alternatives.
counterparty.excerpt_or_summary: Business and scenario artifacts identify dependence on large installed-base monetization and continued revenue-growth assumptions.

draft.mechanism: Index ownership and headline hardware-cycle focus can underweight the durability of services monetization and repurchase-supported per-share compounding.
draft.persistence_reason: The disagreement can persist until services mix, margin resilience, and capital return show up across several reported periods rather than one product cycle.

structural_mispricing.excerpt_or_summary: Moat and capital-allocation drafts support recurring ecosystem economics and shareholder-return behavior.

variant_view.draft: The variant view is that the market is over-indexing on near-term hardware cyclicality and underweighting ecosystem retention, services mix, and disciplined buybacks as per-share value drivers.
variant_view.source_label: C-2 Moat, C-3 CapAlloc, and C-4 scenarios
variant_view.excerpt_or_summary: Filed Analyst artifacts support ecosystem durability, capital return, and scenario drivers.

draft.event: Quarterly services revenue and gross-margin disclosures
draft.why_it_matters: Confirms or breaks the services-mix durability leg of the variant view.

draft.event: Annual capital return authorization and diluted-share trend
draft.why_it_matters: Tests whether per-share compounding remains supported by buybacks without balance-sheet strain.

catalysts.excerpt_or_summary: Capital allocation and valuation artifacts identify buybacks, margins, and revenue growth as thesis drivers.

cruxes.claim: Services mix must keep supporting consolidated economics.
cruxes.threshold_value: stable-to-expanding services contribution with no material gross-margin compression

cruxes.claim: Buybacks must remain accretive rather than masking operating stagnation.
cruxes.threshold_value: diluted shares decline while net leverage remains within filed comfort range

cruxes.claim: The moat case requires sustained customer retention and ecosystem pricing power.
cruxes.threshold_value: management disclosures or operating metrics remain consistent with high retention and attachment

source_evidence_summary.business: Business artifact frames AAPL as an installed-base and ecosystem monetization case.
source_evidence_summary.moat: Moat artifact supports, but does not decide, the durability mechanism.
source_evidence_summary.capalloc: Capital allocation artifact supports buyback and balance-sheet discipline evidence.
source_evidence_summary.scenarios: Scenario artifact identifies filed valuation drivers and Senior-owned probabilities.
source_evidence_summary.mechanical: Gate card, method directive, valuation range, expectations line, and spine provide mechanical context.
